sections.heading: What is Firebase Cloud Messaging?
sections.content: FCM is Google's cross-platform messaging solution for sending push notifications and data messages to Android, iOS, and web apps. Apps receive a unique registration token to target messages. Notification messages are displayed automatically in the background, while data messages are always delivered to FirebaseMessagingService for custom handling.

sections.heading: FCM Message Types
items: Notification — system displays automatically, simple alerts
Data — app handles everything, silent sync or custom UI
Combined — background auto-display plus extra data payload

sections.heading: Setup Steps
items: Create Firebase project, add Android app, download google-services.json
Add Firebase plugin and dependencies in build.gradle
Extend FirebaseMessagingService: override onMessageReceived() and onNewToken()
Declare service in manifest with MESSAGING_EVENT intent filter
Create NotificationChannel on API 26+, request POST_NOTIFICATIONS on API 33+

sections.heading: Notification Handling
items: Set small icon, title, text, PendingIntent for tap
Use unique IDs for different notifications
Show immediately; enqueue WorkManager for heavy processing
Handle tap deep links via intent extras in MainActivity

sections.heading: Best Practices
items: Use data messages for critical payloads
Always update backend with latest FCM token
Create channels once; use proper importance levels
Keep onMessageReceived() fast; delegate heavy tasks

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: FCM allows reliable push notifications with fine-grained control. Use FirebaseMessagingService for message handling, manage tokens carefully, create channels for Android 8+, request permissions on Android 13+, and display notifications with NotificationCompat for full control. Use topics for broadcast messaging and keep processing lightweight in onMessageReceived().
